 {"id":"bd-2vv.1","title":"Symbol expansion: string/wcs family completion","description":"Expand string family coverage from current partial to complete.

Current State (from support_matrix.json):
- mem* family: mostly Implemented
- str* family: partial Implemented
- wcs* family: partial GlibcCallThrough

Target Additions:
- strnlen, strndup, strchrnul, strcasestr
- memrchr, memmem, mempcpy
- stpcpy, stpncpy
- All strerror variants
- All wcs* wide string functions

Implementation Strategy:
1. Audit current coverage: rg 'string_abi' crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/
2. For each missing symbol: implement in frankenlibc-core/src/string/
3. Add membrane integration in frankenlibc-abi
4. Add conformance fixtures
5. Validate strict/hardened behavior

Testing Requirements:
- Unit tests: empty input, max-length input, overlapping regions
- Conformance fixtures: captured from glibc
- E2E: programs using strstr, strcasestr (case-insensitive search)
- Hardened: verify truncation/clamping behavior

Success Criteria:
- All string.h functions classified as Implemented or RawSyscall
- Zero glibc call-through for string family
- All fixtures pass

## Acceptance Criteria

- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.

- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3","title":"Symbol expansion: stdio family (phased approach)","description":"Complete stdio family coverage - the largest and most complex subsystem.

Current State:
- FILE* operations: mostly GlibcCallThrough
- printf family: partial Implemented (format engine exists)
- scanf family: likely stubs
- fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite: GlibcCallThrough

Target Architecture:
FrankenLibC needs its own FILE structure and buffering:
1. FILE state machine (read/write/append modes)
2. Buffer management (fully buffered, line buffered, unbuffered)
3. Error and EOF flag handling
4. Wide character support (fwide, fwprintf)

Implementation Phases:
Phase 1: Core FILE operations
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, fgets, fputs

Phase 2: Formatted I/O
- printf family (fprintf, sprintf, snprintf, asprintf)
- scanf family (fscanf, sscanf)

Phase 3: Positioning
- fseek, ftell, fgetpos, fsetpos
- rewind

Phase 4: Misc
- freopen, fdopen, fileno
- setvbuf, setbuf
- clearerr, feof, ferror

Testing:
- Unit: each FILE operation in isolation
- Integration: multi-step file processing workflows
- Concurrency: thread-safe FILE operations
- Hardened: handling of invalid FILE pointers

Complexity Warning:
This is ~20% of glibc by LOC. Needs phased approach.

Success Criteria:
- All stdio.h functions classified Implemented
- Zero glibc call-through for stdio
- Python hello world works (heavy stdio user)

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.1","title":"Stdio Phase 1: FILE structure and basic I/O","description":"Stdio Phase 1: Core FILE structure and basic operations.

Goal: Implement FrankenLibC's own FILE structure with basic I/O.

FILE Structure Design:
- File descriptor (int fd)
- Buffer pointer and size
- Buffer position (read/write)
- Flags (read/write/append/binary)
- Error and EOF flags
- Lock for thread safety
- Wide orientation flag

Phase 1 Functions:
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, ungetc
- fgets, fputs
- fileno

Buffer Management:
- Full buffering (_IOFBF)
- Line buffering (_IOLBF)
- No buffering (_IONBF)
- setvbuf, setbuf

Standard Streams:
- stdin, stdout, stderr initialization
- Connection to fd 0, 1, 2

Testing:
- Unit: each operation in isolation
- Integration: read/write round-trips
- Concurrency: concurrent access to same FILE
- Error: invalid FILE pointer handling

Success Criteria:
- Basic file I/O works
- Buffer management correct
- Thread safety maintained

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.2","title":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family","description":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family implementation.

Goal: Complete printf format engine.

Printf Functions:
- printf, fprintf, sprintf, snprintf
- vprintf, vfprintf, vsprintf, vsnprintf
- asprintf, vasprintf (GNU extensions)
- dprintf, vdprintf

Format Specifiers:
- Integer: d, i, u, o, x, X, b (C23)
- Float: f, F, e, E, g, G, a, A
- Character: c, s, p, n, %
- Width and precision: *, digit
- Flags: -, +, space, #, 0
- Length modifiers: hh, h, l, ll, j, z, t, L

Implementation Strategy:
Format engine in frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs:
1. Parse format string
2. Extract arguments from va_list
3. Format each specifier
4. Output to destination

Complexity Points:
- Floating-point formatting (Ryu or Dragonbox)
- Positional arguments ($)
- Wide character output (wprintf family)

Testing:
- Unit: each specifier in isolation
- Edge cases: width overflow, precision truncation
- Locale: decimal separator (depends on locale)
- Security: %n restrictions in hardened mode

Success Criteria:
- All specifiers implemented
- Output matches glibc format
- Hardened mode restricts %n

 {"id":"bd-3een","title":"LCRQ ring queues for IPC (section 14.6)","description":"Implement LCRQ (Linked Concurrent Ring Queue) for high-performance inter-process communication in FrankenLibC. LCRQ combines the cache-friendliness of ring buffers with the unbounded capacity of linked lists. Structure: a linked list of fixed-size ring segments. Each segment is a cache-line-aligned array with head/tail indices using FAA (Fetch-And-Add) for O(1) enqueue/dequeue. When a segment fills, a new one is linked. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Evidence ledger IPC — when the evidence ledger runs in a separate process (for crash isolation), LCRQ provides the high-throughput, low-latency communication channel. Target: >10M events/sec with <100ns per-event latency. (2) Deferred free IPC — in hardened mode, freed blocks may be sent to a separate quarantine manager process. LCRQ handles the transfer without blocking the application thread. (3) Metric export — real-time allocation statistics exported to monitoring via LCRQ. Implementation: (a) LcrqSegment<T, N> — ring buffer of N slots. (b) Lcrq<T, N> — linked list of segments with hazard-pointer-based reclamation for consumed segments. (c) Shared memory support — LCRQ must work across processes via mmap'd shared memory (all pointers must be offset-based, not absolute). (d) Backpressure — when the consumer falls behind, the producer sees a full queue and applies configurable backpressure (block, drop, or signal).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.6 of graveyard. LCRQ from Morrison & Afek (2013 PPoPP). Linked ring queue combining CRQ segments for unbounded MPMC queues. Hazard pointer reclamation from Michael (2004).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LcrqSegment<T, const N: usize>: ring buffer of N slots, head/tail as AtomicU64 using fetch_add.
- Lcrq<T, const N: usize>: linked list of segments. New segment allocated when current fills.
- Slot: enum { Empty, Full(T), Invalid }. CAS-based enqueue/dequeue on individual slots.
- Shared memory: offset-based pointers (usize offsets from base) for cross-process use via mmap.
- Hazard pointer: reclaim consumed segments after grace period (integrate with RCU from bd-3aof if available).
- Backpressure: BackpressurePolicy enum { Block, Drop, Signal } configurable per queue instance.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. LCRQ passes MPMC correctness test: 16 producers + 16 consumers, 1M items, zero lost items.
2. Throughput >10M items/sec for 64-byte payloads on single-socket system.
3. Per-item latency <100ns at p99 under moderate load (50% utilization).
4. Shared memory mode: cross-process producer/consumer works correctly (test with fork()).
5. Backpressure: Block mode blocks producer when consumer falls behind, Drop mode drops with counter, Signal mode sends SIGUSR2.
6. Segment reclamation: no memory leak after 10M enqueue/dequeue cycles.
7. Miri clean for single-threaded correctness path.

- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: lcrq, operation=enqueue|dequeue, segment_id, slot_index, backpressure) per operation.

 {"id":"bd-3fb","title":"EPIC: Extreme Optimization Loop (baselines + profiles + goldens)","description":"Enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop for allocator + membrane:
BASELINE -> PROFILE -> PROVE -> IMPLEMENT (one lever) -> VERIFY -> REPEAT.

Deliverables:
- Baseline suite (hyperfine + criterion).
- Flamegraph/profiler playbook.
- Golden outputs + isomorphism proof template wired into PR process.

Acceptance:
- We can prove behavior unchanged after optimizations via goldens/checksums.
- Perf regressions are detected quickly and reproducibly.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.

- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.189454921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023072672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023028389Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:40:06.776846377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-1ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.057923860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.269519020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-26o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.732163604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.945025655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2r0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.163975230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.622778174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.845997280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fil","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_fault reusable fault injection","description":"Integrate with franken_fault for reusable fault injection: concurrency (cancellation at worst points, ABA hazards, starvation), time (virtual time drift), memory (OOM, corruption injection). - Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.

- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.281414666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:20.446682025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","fault-injection","frankenlibc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fil","depends_on_id":"bd-2hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3g4p","title":"Support matrix: automated maintenance","description":"Automated support matrix maintenance.

Goal: Keep support_matrix.json synchronized with code reality.

Automation Components:

1. Symbol Discovery
   - Parse exported symbols from libfrankenlibc_abi.so
   - Compare against support_matrix.json
   - Report new/removed symbols

2. Status Validation
   - For Implemented: verify no todo!/unimplemented!
   - For RawSyscall: verify syscall veneer usage
   - For GlibcCallThrough: verify glibc call
   - For Stub: verify deterministic fallback

3. Conformance Linkage
   - Each symbol links to fixture files
   - Each symbol links to test commands
   - Coverage report generation

4. Drift Detection
   - CI gate fails on drift
   - PR comments with drift summary
   - Automated fix suggestions

5. Report Generation
   - reality_report.v1.json from support_matrix
   - Feature parity reports
   - Coverage dashboards

CI Integration:
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on drift
- Generate coverage reports

Success Criteria:
- Zero manual maintenance of support_matrix
- Drift detected within one PR
- Reports always current

## Acceptance Criteria
